file.content:
Turn q until it lines up with a key.

q = {queryAngle} deg

{#each scores as s (s.id)}
{s.id}
{fmt(s.score)}
{/each}

content.text:
The component at the target path already implements the full spec correctly: an amber query vector driven by a custom `role="slider"` dial (pointer-capture drag plus 5-degree arrow-key steps, Home/End), four fixed periwinkle key vectors labeled at their tips, and four lilac score bars with mono dot-product readouts (cosine similarity) that rise/fall live and pivot around a baseline so anti-alignment reads as clearly as alignment. I verified the math against all four driven states (90, 30, 20, 200 degrees): k1 peaks at 1.00 exactly at 20 degrees, bottoms at -1.00 at 200 degrees, and the "leading" bar correctly shifts to whichever key the query is closest to at each angle. All colors route through the specified tokens, labels/readouts use mono font, touch target and reduced-motion rules are satisfied, and there are no raw hex values or dash characters.

DONE Verified and confirmed C:\Users\jaker\Desktop\projects\harnesses\lessons\sessions\slice-attention\page\interactives\QkExplorer.svelte: a draggable/keyboard-operable query-angle dial with four fixed key vectors and four live lilac score bars (mono dot-product readouts) that rise toward the best-aligned key and fall as the query turns away, matching all four spec states.
